skill.name: google-agents-cli-observability
skill.description: This skill should be used when the user wants to "set up tracing", "monitor my ADK agent", "configure logging", "add observability", "debug production traffic", or needs guidance on monitoring deployed ADK (Agent Development Kit) agents. Covers Cloud Trace, prompt-response logging, BigQuery Agent Analytics, third-party integrations (AgentOps, Phoenix, MLflow, etc.), and troubleshooting. Part of the Google ADK (Agent Development Kit) skills suite. Do NOT use for deployment setup (use google-agents-cli-deploy) or API code patterns (use google-agents-cli-adk-code).
skill.summary: Guides setup of tracing, logging, and analytics for deployed ADK agents using Cloud Trace, BigQuery, and third-party platforms.
skill.author: google

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 121 static findings against actual file content. All findings are false positives: backtick detections triggered on environment variable names in markdown tables (e.g., OTEL_INSTRUMENTATION_GENAI_CAPTURE_MESSAGE_CONTENT), URL detections are documentation links to Google's official adk.dev site, and crypto detections triggered on unrelated text patterns. This is a pure documentation skill with no executable code, no prompt injection attempts, and no security concerns.

content.user_title: Monitor and Debug ADK Agents in Production
content.value_statement: Deploying AI agents without observability makes debugging failures nearly impossible. This skill provides structured guidance for adding tracing, logging, and analytics to Google ADK agent deployments.
actual_capabilities: Configure Cloud Trace for distributed tracing of ADK agent invocations
Set up prompt-response logging with privacy-preserving metadata capture
Enable BigQuery Agent Analytics plugin for structured event logging
Integrate third-party observability platforms (AgentOps, Phoenix, MLflow, Arize, Weave)
Troubleshoot missing traces, logging failures, and telemetry configuration issues
Guide Terraform-based infrastructure provisioning for telemetry resources
limitations: Does not handle deployment setup or CI/CD pipeline configuration
Does not cover ADK Python API code patterns or agent implementation
Requires Terraform-provisioned infrastructure for prompt-response logging and BigQuery analytics
Third-party platform integrations require separate provider-specific credentials

use_cases.title: Set up initial tracing for a new ADK deployment
use_cases.description: A developer just deployed their first ADK agent and needs to add observability. This skill guides them through enabling Cloud Trace and configuring prompt-response logging.
use_cases.target_user: ADK developers new to production deployments

use_cases.title: Debug latency issues in production agent traffic
use_cases.description: An SRE notices slow response times from a deployed agent. This skill helps interpret Cloud Trace spans to identify bottlenecks in LLM calls or tool executions.
use_cases.target_user: Site reliability engineers monitoring AI services

use_cases.title: Evaluate third-party observability platforms
use_cases.description: A team lead needs to choose between AgentOps, Phoenix, MLflow, and other platforms. This skill presents trade-offs and setup complexity for each option.

prompt_templates.title: Basic tracing setup
prompt_templates.prompt: I just deployed my ADK agent to Agent Runtime. How do I verify Cloud Trace is capturing my agent invocations?

prompt_templates.title: Enable prompt-response logging
prompt_templates.prompt: Walk me through enabling prompt-response logging for my deployed ADK agent. I need to capture LLM interactions for auditing but want metadata-only mode for privacy.

prompt_templates.title: Configure BigQuery Agent Analytics
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to log structured agent events to BigQuery for building custom dashboards. How do I enable the BQ Agent Analytics plugin and what infrastructure do I need?

prompt_templates.title: Compare third-party observability platforms
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare AgentOps, Phoenix, and MLflow for monitoring my ADK agent. I need session replay capabilities and team collaboration features. Which is best for a 5-person ML team?

output_examples.input: User asks: 'How do I check if prompt-response logging is working after deployment?'
output: Guide user to verify GCS bucket has completion files using gsutil ls
Check BigQuery log export table for inference operation records
Query the completions view that joins log export with GCS data
Explain that log export to BigQuery may take a few minutes to propagate

output_examples.input: User asks: 'What's the difference between NO_CONTENT and span_and_event modes?'
output: Explain that NO_CONTENT captures only metadata (tokens, timing, model name)
Clarify that span_and_event captures full message content in spans and events
Note that the scaffolded setup_telemetry() collapses any non-false value to NO_CONTENT for privacy
Recommend NO_CONTENT for production with sensitive data, span_and_event for debugging

best_practices: Run agents-cli infra single-project before first deploy to avoid Terraform state mismatches with agent_runtime deployments
Use NO_CONTENT mode for prompt-response logging in production to protect sensitive conversation data
Combine Cloud Trace (always-on) with optional tiers based on debugging needs and compliance requirements
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for deployment setup or CI/CD configuration (use google-agents-cli-deploy instead)
Avoid enabling full content capture in production without reviewing data privacy implications
Do not skip the scaffold step when using BigQuery Analytics, as Terraform provisions required infrastructure automatically

faq.question: Does Cloud Trace work without any configuration?
faq.answer: Yes. Cloud Trace is always enabled and exports OpenTelemetry spans automatically for all deployment types including local development with agents-cli playground.

faq.question: What infrastructure do I need for prompt-response logging?
faq.answer: You need Terraform-provisioned resources: a GCS logs bucket, BigQuery dataset with external tables, and log sinks. Run agents-cli infra single-project to provision these.

faq.question: How do I disable telemetry in production?
faq.answer: Set OTEL_INSTRUMENTATION_GENAI_CAPTURE_MESSAGE_CONTENT=false in your Terraform service configuration and re-apply. This disables content capture while keeping trace metadata.

faq.question: Can I use third-party observability platforms alongside Cloud Trace?
faq.answer: Yes, but some platforms like AgentOps replace native telemetry. Check provider documentation for setup specifics and potential conflicts.

faq.question: Why are my BigQuery analytics events not appearing?
faq.answer: Verify the plugin is configured in app/agent.py and that BQ_ANALYTICS_DATASET_ID environment variable is set. Log export via sinks may take several minutes to propagate.

faq.question: What is the BigQuery dataset naming convention?
faq.answer: Dataset names cannot contain hyphens. Terraform converts hyphens in your project name to underscores, so project my-agent becomes dataset my_agent_telemetry.
